rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARIYA LYUBLINA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT CLAUDIO A. BLUER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued to March 16, 2023 to be heard with the other pending motion. Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the opposition to the motion with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (Moving papers received) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: NAIFEH AZAR VS. MARIYA LYUBLINA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Special Motion To Strike Portions Of Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT CLAUDIO A. BLUER'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT is continued to March 16, 2023. Moving party to file a supplemental brief not to exceed 2 pages specifying which part of CCP 425.16 is applicable to the facts of this case: (e)(1), (e)(2), (e)(3) or (e)(4). Moving party shall also include any factually similar ANTI-SLAPP cases re: inspection reports. If no such cases are located, moving party to state "none." Supplemental brief to be filed no later than March 6, 2023. Plaintiff may file a reply not to exceed two pages no later than March 13, 2023. No later than March 3, 2023 Plaintiff to provide courtesy copies of the opposition to the motion with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. (Moving papers and reply received). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DANIEL GALA ET AL VS. DENNIS LOUIE, INDIVIDUALLY, AND AS TRUSTEE OF THE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Of Claims
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2023 line 3. DEFENDANTS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF CLAIMS is DENIED. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1350(b) - compare Notice of Motion and Separate Statement. Furthermore, multiple triable issues of fact exist, including, but not limited to 13, 21, 27, 28, 44 and 53. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: WONG FAMILY BENEVOLENT ASSOCIATION VS. SAMUEL WONG ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for March 2, 2023 line 7. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to properly verify the complaint per CCP 446. Plaintiff reliance on form UD-100 is misplaced. The form on its face states: "use a different verification form if the verification is by an attorney or for a corporation or partnership." Plaintiff does not address these express instructions. Demurer is further sustained with leave to amend on uncertainty grounds for Plaintiff to reconcile the Notice giving Defendants 5 full days to vacate with allegations in the complaint giving defendants only until noon (11:59 a.m.) on the 5th day to vacate. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Notice is not uncertain, and Ds failed to show that all causes of action asserted in the complaint require a valid notice as an element of the cause of action. Defendants demur to the entire complaint and not to individual causes of action. (See Notice of Demurrer and Demurrer). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
